column3: Cache-Control headers are correctly set (no-cache, no-store). ZAP flags this on the unauthenticated login page which intentionally prevents caching.
X-Frame-Options: DENY is set by Spring Security. ZAP incorrectly flags this on OAuth redirect responses where the header is not included.
Content-Security-Policy is set by Spring Security. ZAP incorrectly flags this on OAuth redirect responses where the header is not included.
Non-storable 302 redirects to Azure AD OAuth are expected and intentional. These are authentication redirects that must not be cached.
Base64 values are CSRF tokens and OAuth state parameters in authentication redirect URLs. These are expected and do not represent information disclosure.
Unix timestamps are OAuth state parameters in authentication redirect URLs. These are expected and do not represent information disclosure.
Informational notice only - ZAP identifying this as a modern web application. Not a vulnerability.
Informational notice only - ZAP identifying session management cookies. Session cookies are correctly configured with HttpOnly, Secure and SameSite=Lax.
ZAP's own scanner does not send the Sec-Fetch-Dest header. This is a false positive about ZAP's own behaviour, not the server.
